item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Not as good the original, but these can be good alternatives:

+ show block
– hide block

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Wild_%26_red_hair_day.jpg

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:%D7%A0%D7%A2%D7%A8%D7%94_%D7%92%27%D7%99%D7%A0%D7%92%27%D7%99%D7%AA.JPG

Collapsed the post so the link wouldn't stretch the screen. ~Zyn

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image is a stock image originating from here which requires buying a license in order to use the image for commercial uses. As such, it is not compliant with CC 3.0 and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a very interesting idea, and well executed, too.

The article fails to stick the landing, instead spend its last quarter doing the "anomaly is upset about being contained, the Foundation is mean" song and dance. Having read this exact same thing roughly ten thousand times, it did nothing for me. No vote.

item.title: Apologies, highest necromqncer.
item.content:encoded: So her effect is leaking: other versions of herself from different realities slip through her when she dies in that respective way in a different reality, and those versions take her place. But I'm guessing (plot point from 2084) shifted her to her respective clone, instead of leaving her trapped in the other anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm impressed by the sheer number of different SCP tropes whose boundaries this veers dangerously close to, without ever quite falling in.

  • She's a humanoid SCP with a disturbing involuntary anomalous effect… but she's adapted to it and she feels in control, or at least comfortable.
  • She calls the Foundation on their dehumanizing and unethical shit in an interview… but only to justify her own behavior, not to condemn theirs.
  • She understands the way the Foundation sees her and their intentions for her future, and she's manipulating them into treating her a certain way… but only enough to make them keep her around, not to escape or to hoodwink them for some larger purpose.
  • She gets unexpectedly violent toward the end of the article… but neither she nor any D-Class or other Foundation personnel are harmed, and the incident merits only a minor change to her containment procedures.
  • She's clearly neither cooperating with the Foundation, nor confidently biding her time, nor attempting escape whenever possible, nor resigned to her fate. That alone feels like a revelation compared to the standard humanoid SCP archetypes.

item.title: Girls got some balls
item.content:encoded:
Backsassing Foundation personnel doesnt seem like a good idea to me, especially if she's a keter-class SCP who is dangerous to the public and prone to psychotic breaks. Uncooperation generally leads to termination, Like with 531-D
http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/termination-order

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know this is just random,

But I swear, she's the spy from TF2 using a dead ringer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Breach contained, great success!
Breach contained, great success!

thanks to the artist Jenssosaurus

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: she is exactly right though….i also figured they could use copies like D Class….but it might need some rewrite for it work…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how it sets up an assumption of simple copying but then implies alternate versions of 2288 are being ripped from other dimensions and dumped in our world to be murdered by the Foundation. Changes it from disposal of effects of an SCP to straight up murder of total innocents. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Completely pointless and noninformative response to your speculation
– hide block

SCP-2084

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So…is…this a quantum immortal whose alternate-world "corpses" wind up appearing next to her after each branching point?

+ Speculation about the significance of the textbooks
- ENGAGE SPECULATRON 9000!

Hrm, the books about China all involve the 1600s, while the one on life in medieval Europe would be roughly the 5th to 15th century…the main thing sticking out about the books on Chinese history is the one mentioning the history of the "Qing Empire" from the 1600s to the modern day. Unless the last chapters are discussing repercussions that occurred in later decades from the collapse of Qing - which is fully possible - then the -B instance must be from a parallel reality, since the Qing/Manchu dynasty collapsed in 1912 (though there was apparently a failed attempt at restoration of the dynasty in 1917), leading to the formation of the Republic of China.

Further evidence towards the parallel reality theory is that the flag of the Qing dynasty bore a blue dragon, not a red one (though admittedly the book could be using "red" to refer to communism, which would certainly apply to the People's Republic of China that succeeded the Republic of China in 1949.)

Of course, I could be completely off base with that speculation…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to upvote anyway because it's a great entry, but the mental picture of an infinitely increasing mountain of corpses at Site-███ just makes it perfect. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed it, particularly for the interview log. This one feels more like the Foundation environment is having a meaningful effect on her character than most other sentient SCPs. She's not just becoming hostile; she's adapting to a hostile environment in a way that feels like she's developing an antisocial personality disorder.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I recall posting regarding the previous version.

I was more able to understand this new version, as clarifications were given where necessary, and I liked the interview. I recall complaining last time that I didn't understand the "coming away" process of the "A" subjects from the original, but given it's a quantum space-time thing, I've realized since then that it would be rather difficult to actually attempt to explain, and I can imagine it a bit better now.

Your article has improved; my personal tilt is higher, and my understanding issues are more or less resolved, so I see no reason to not give you a +1 for this (I believe my stand on the previous version was a temporary no-vote, if I recall correctly); you now have my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a lot better than last time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This anomaly was first discovered when SCP-2288 suffered a serious injury on-campus, when an instance of SCP-2288-A emerged as students moved in to aid and re-orient the subject

I was a little confused by the phrasing of this sentence. It kind of implies that the serious injury was caused by "an instance of SCP-2288-A emerg[ing] as students moved in to aid and re-orient the subject." For clarity's sake, you might want to change it to something like: "This anomaly was discovered when SCP-2288 suffered a serious injury on campus; as students moved in to aid and re-orient the subject, the first instance of SCP-2288-A emerged."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: She could benefit from some 447 into her life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Clarification: This SCP is an augmented version I had posted months ago which failed. It ended just with the discovery of -B and the textbooks. It also had versions in which she could force the duplications by will sometimes living copies, and a spiffy scene with her killing a bunch of herselves and fashioning a big-ass spear from their legbones.

The version I posted two days ago was basically completely different, but still with the duplicationings. That version will eventually be re-uploaded after being rewritten when I figure out what to do with it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is significantly better. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This version is far, far better! I'm glad you managed to take the old work and turn it into something great. Great job, Andariel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this version. Good job, Andariel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Completely different story and sufficiently different anomaly. Still like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember this from last time. The tone is much, much better. I like this. The interview came out really well also. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Concurred. The picture is pretty horrible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding general complaint about the picture; I didn't get a chance to comment on it earlier. Otherwise pretty good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
=(

I'll look in to changing that soon. Should be easy enough, teenagers all look the same anyway

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you finally got this one working. I liked it before, and I like it even more now, especially with the amount of personality you gave her.

Not a fan of the picture, though. It feels like you were going for "pissed-off mugshot" but it looks more like some girl who's into bondage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was worth the effort you went through to get this back up. It definitely shows.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rewrite of my original "Copy of A" posted a few months ago and did not succeed. I added stuff to make it awesomer
